scenario: Harbor Insurance is migrating 12 Camunda 8.8 Spring Boot services that together process 75000 claims per day. The platform team is reviewing the camunda.client.auth.method property and must confirm the exact enum values the 8.8 properties reference admits before locking down its YAML profiles.
question: Which set of values is documented for the camunda.client.auth.method enum in the Camunda 8.8 properties reference?

options.text: The reference lists camunda.client.auth.method as enum[none, basic, oidc] under the camunda.client.auth section of the 8.8 Spring Boot starter.
The reference lists camunda.client.auth.method as enum[none, basic, saml] under the camunda.client.auth section of the 8.8 Spring Boot starter.
The reference lists camunda.client.auth.method as enum[none, basic, oauth2] under the camunda.client.auth section of the 8.8 Spring Boot starter.
The reference lists camunda.client.auth.method as enum[none, oidc, mtls] under the camunda.client.auth section of the 8.8 Spring Boot starter.

a.text: Correct. The 8.8 properties reference documents camunda.client.auth.method with type enum[none, basic, oidc]; the value is detected automatically when not set.

b.text: Incorrect. SAML is not one of the documented enum values; the third option in the 8.8 reference is oidc.

c.text: Incorrect. The reference uses the token oidc rather than oauth2 for the third enum value of camunda.client.auth.method.

d.text: Incorrect. The enum does not include mtls; the documented values are none, basic, and oidc on the 8.8 Spring Boot starter.

explanation: The properties reference documents camunda.client.auth.method as enum[none, basic, oidc]. When unset, the method is inferred from the presence of username, password, client ID, and client secret.

docs.title: Camunda Spring Boot starter properties reference
